D6 Display L4 Analyze Reference

Backlight: Edge vs Array

an edge-lit LCD can't make a true black; a full-array zoned backlight can — but it blooms a halo around bright objects

An LCD blocks a backlight; it cannot truly switch off. An edge-lit panel shares one light across the whole screen, so dark areas glow grey. Full-array local dimming (FALD) splits the backlight into zones that dim independently for deeper blacks — but a small bright object lights its whole zone, haloing the surrounding darkness (blooming).

A bright star sits on black. Switch to edge-lit and the whole field lifts to grey. Switch to array and raise the zone count: blacks deepen, but watch the blocky bloom around the star shrink only as the zones get finer.
